title: Nervous System Reorganization and Integrated Consciousness

concepts.title: Observation Without Identification as Operative Stance
concepts.description: the conscious stance toward activated poles; feeling the impulse without being captured by it; four practical tests for identification; neurobiological substrate (window of tolerance); maintenance through practice

concepts.title: Integrated Consciousness Under Activation: Nervous System Capacity
concepts.description: maintaining both consciousness and activation simultaneously; prefrontal-limbic coordination as neurobiological requirement; six operational signatures; maintenance cost; the limits of integrated consciousness; cross-domain parallels to Eastern enlightenment and Gigerenzer immanent reflection

concepts.title: Sacred Container as Operational Architecture
concepts.description: four operational requirements (genuine boundary, clear challenge, competent authority, integration ritual); how containers remove competing activations; nervous system reorganization mechanism; operational advantage; costs and implementation complexity

concepts.title: Relational Trust and Network Power
concepts.description: integrated operators build relational infrastructure that compounds over time; shadow-specialized operators win single transactions but eventually isolate; network as selection environment; mathematical advantage in repeated games; redemption limitation for early shadow specialization

concepts.title: Boundary Setting and Tactical Positioning
concepts.description: psychological register (genuine development through integration) vs. behavioral-mechanics register (constructed performance); recognition tests; vulnerability of constructed boundaries

concepts.title: Controlled Aggression as Operational Discipline
concepts.description: natural development (four phases) vs. performed discipline; integration test; neurological substrate differences; collapse under novel stress

concepts.title: Intermittent Reinforcement and Control Dynamics
concepts.description: natural emergence vs. deliberate construction; variable patterns as control mechanism; resistance to extinction; addiction architecture

concepts.title: Mercenary vs. Warrior: The Mission Distinction as Operational Reality
concepts.description: operational signatures showing consciousness; decision-making, accountability, development, truth-telling patterns; succession crisis dynamics

concepts.title: Sadomasochistic Control Dynamics and Leverage
concepts.description: natural emergence vs. deliberate construction; behavioral techniques for building dominant position; isolation, oscillation, shame-binding; ethical dimension

concepts.title: Authority Dynamics and the Construction of Deference
concepts.description: earned authority (psychological) vs. constructed authority (behavioral); recognition tests; ethical problem of authority without accountability

concepts.title: Power Presence and Charisma as Behavioral Technology
concepts.description: genuine presence from integration vs. performed presence from technique; vulnerability under novel stress; nervous system signature differences
